content: According to 2 Thessalonians 1:1, who joined Paul as co-authors of this letter?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:1 — 'Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, to the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.'

content: To which church is 2 Thessalonians addressed?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:1 — '...to the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.' The church at Thessalonica, capital of Macedonia.

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 1:3, for what does Paul thank God concerning the believers?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:3 — '...because your faith is growing abundantly, and the love of every one of you for one another is increasing.'
options: Their faith growing abundantly and love increasing for one another
Their large numbers of new converts

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 1:7, when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven, with whom does He come?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:7 — '...when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels in flaming fire...'
options: With the resurrected Old Testament prophets
With His mighty angels in flaming fire

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 1:8, the Lord inflicts vengeance on those who do what?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:8 — '...inflicting vengeance on those who do not know God and on those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.' Two omissions — not knowing God and not obeying the gospel.
options: Do not know God and do not obey the gospel
Do not keep the Sabbath strictly

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 1:9, what is the punishment of those who do not obey?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:9 — 'They will suffer the punishment of eternal destruction, away from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his might.'
options: Imprisonment for a time, then release
Eternal destruction, away from the presence of the Lord
Reformation and restoration after a thousand years

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 2:1, what events does Paul address as approaching?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:1 — '...concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to him...' Two themes: His return and the gathering of the saints.
options: The coming of the Lord Jesus and our being gathered to Him

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 2:3, the day of the Lord will not come until what happens?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:3 — '...for that day will not come, unless the rebellion comes first, and the man of lawlessness is revealed, the son of destruction.' Two signs — the rebellion and the revealing of the man of lawlessness.
options: The rebellion comes first, and the man of lawlessness (son of destruction) is revealed

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 2:4, what does the man of lawlessness do regarding himself?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:4 — '...who opposes and exalts himself against every so-called god or object of worship, so that he takes his seat in the temple of God, proclaiming himself to be God.'
options: Proclaims himself to be God, sits in the temple of God, exalts himself above every god
Hides in the wilderness to pray
Holds a thanksgiving feast for God
Persuades people to believe in God
tags: Man of lawlessness proclaims himself God

content: What rule does Paul teach in 2 Thessalonians 3:10?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:10 — '...If anyone is not willing to work, let him not eat.' This rule corrects those living in idleness within the church.
options: If anyone is not willing to work, let him not eat
Every believer must go on a mission
Believers must support all the Lord's servants
tags: If not willing to work, do not eat

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 2:8, by what will the Lord Jesus destroy the lawless one?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:8 — '...the Lord Jesus will kill with the breath of his mouth and bring to nothing by the appearance of his coming.'
options: By the breath of His mouth and the appearance of His coming

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 2:9, by whose power does the lawless one come, and what accompanies him?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:9 — 'The coming of the lawless one is by the activity of Satan with all power and false signs and wonders...'
options: By God's power, accompanied by righteousness
By angelic power, accompanied by prayer
By human power, accompanied by battles
By Satan's power, with all false signs and wonders

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 2:11, why does God send a strong delusion?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:10-11 — '...they refused to love the truth and so be saved. Therefore God sends them a strong delusion, so that they may believe what is false...' Refusal of truth opens the door to delusion.
options: God sends delusion so they believe what is false

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 2:13, for what did God choose them from the beginning?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:13 — '...God chose you as the firstfruits to be saved, through sanctification by the Spirit and belief in the truth.'
options: Salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and belief in the truth

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 2:15, what does Paul exhort the brothers to do?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:15 — 'So then, brothers, stand firm and hold to the traditions that you were taught by us, either by our spoken word or by our letter.'
options: Stand firm and hold to the traditions taught

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 3:1, what does Paul ask the believers to pray for?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:1 — 'Finally, brothers, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may speed ahead and be honored, as happened among you.'
options: That Paul may grow rich to help the poor
That Paul may be released from prison
That Paul may become an official apostle
That the word of the Lord may speed ahead and be honored
tags: Word of the Lord speeds ahead

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 3:13, in what should believers not grow weary?
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:13 — 'As for you, brothers, do not grow weary in doing good.' A short exhortation — keep doing good even when surrounded by idle people.
tags: Do not grow weary in doing good

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 1:8-9, what is the fate of those who do not obey the gospel? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:8-9 — 'They will suffer the punishment of eternal destruction, away from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his might.' Three clear elements; 'instantly annihilated' contradicts 'eternal' punishment.
options: Suffer the punishment of eternal destruction
Away from the presence of the Lord
Away from the glory of His might

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 2:9-10, what marks the coming of the lawless one? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:9-10 — '...by the activity of Satan with all power and false signs and wonders, and with all wicked deception for those who are perishing...' Three marks; 'plainly seen by all' contradicts the deceptive nature.
options: All power, false signs, and wonders
All wicked deception for those perishing

content: According to 2 Thessalonians 3:7-9, what example did Paul set when he was with the Thessalonians? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:7-9 — '...we were not idle when we were with you, nor did we eat anyone's bread without paying for it, but with toil and labor we worked night and day...' Three things; 'living luxuriously' contradicts the entire passage.
options: Not eating anyone's bread without paying
Toiling night and day, not burdening anyone
